title: Record: SP4096 + Int6 QAT + NorMuon (val_bpb=1.2012)
body:
## Summary

- SP4096 tokenizer (26% better text compression than sp1024)
- Int6 STE QAT + zstd-22 compression (fits in 14.3MB)
- NorMuon optimizer + tuned learning rates

## Result

**val_bpb = 1.2012** on 8xH100 SXM, 600s training, 14.3MB artifact.

Beats baseline (1.2244) by 0.023 BPB.

## Key Metrics

| Metric | Value |
|--------|-------|
| val_bpb (post-quant) | 1.2012 |
| Artifact size | 14,342,773 bytes |
| Steps | 11,497 |
| Hardware | 8xH100 SXM 80GB |

## Test plan

- [x] Train sp4096 tokenizer and verify compression ratio
- [x] Local MLX smoke test
- [x] RTX 5090 10-min comparison (16 configs tested)
- [x] 8xH100 RunPod validation (600s)
- [x] Artifact under 16,000,000 bytes
- [x] train.log attached

comments.body: Would be nice to have the tokenizer and dataset somehow available to replicate. Also, this most likely wont scale up as the baseline compressed size is very close to the limit.

Thanks for the feedback! Good points on both.

**Tokenizer and dataset:** I'll upload the sp4096 tokenizer and pre-tokenized shards to HuggingFace so anyone can replicate. Will add the link to the README.

**Compressed size:** You're right that the baseline config (9L/512d tied) lands at ~13.6MB, which leaves headroom but not a ton. We've been experimenting with untied embeddings (15.0MB, still under 16MB) which actually gives the best BPB so far in our single-GPU tests. The budget is tight but workable -- and the tokenizer improvement alone is worth ~12% BPB even without architectural changes.

Still working on the 8xH100 validation run. Will update the PR with final numbers and the dataset link soon.
